task: Explore ~/nyro-mod WITHOUT git (it is not a git repo). Report: (1) full file/dir tree (ls -la recursively but skip huge binaries), (2) what is nyro-src/ — does it contain source code? List its top-level structure, (3) what are nyro-server-fixed, nyro-desktop.backup.original, Nyro.app.original, nyro-debug-findings.md, issues/ — brief purpose of each, (4) any obvious CUSTOM modifications the user made vs an original Nyro release (look for patched files, fixed scripts, custom configs). Read key files. Focus on identifying what the user's "customizations" actually are so we can preserve them during an upstream merge.
